author: kimiwu
content: Hi everyone, I am Kimi. I‘d like to work on `Ream: Minimal PeerDAS Data Availability Client` . I've been looking into it for the last week and already drafted a plan. I'm gonna dive in this week. Cheers!

author: cristian_1337
content:
Hi everyone, I’m Cristian. Very excited to meet you all!

This week I’m planning to take a deeper look into the proposed projects for the cohort with the goal of exploring underlying topics that I haven’t had the chance to take a look on yet. My goal is to gain more familiarity with each of these subjects before committing to a specific project. I’m currently most interested by the improvements included in the Hegota hard fork, like FOCIL (EIP-7805) and Optional Execution Proofs ( EIP-8025), and also interested in the Decentralized CL checkpoint sync idea. I will dive deeper into these subjects through the week.

On the side, I started setting up Lighthouse CL with Reth EL locally, and I will continue the setup as part of my goal to check the current state of implementation, wrt the above, across multiple CL clients. My plan is to also check and setup Prysm.

Excited to get started! Good luck to everyone!

author: bubbly_gull_18605
content:
Hi everyone, I’m Jack. I plan to focus on EIP-7688: Stable Containers. I’ve already started reading the related code, and I’m happy to discuss ideas with anyone interested in the same topic! 

Very excited to start this project!

author: vivek_singh_solanki
content:
Hi everyone, I'm Vivek. Good to be here.

I'm spending this first stretch going through the project-ideas list properly and doing the background reading before I commit, because I'd rather pick something I can genuinely own for the full 5 months than jump on the most popular option. My interests are on the execution-layer side, so that's the direction I'm leaning.

I'm also weighing whether to propose my own project instead of only picking from the list, if I come across a gap that's worth it.

I'll post a more concrete update on GitHub once I've narrowed it down. Looking forward to working with you all.

author: villagefarmer
content: I’m also interested in this topic and already have some local commits. If a few others join in, I think we could knock this out pretty quickly.

author: rahulbarmann
content:
Hi everyone, I'm Rahul. Glad to be here.
I recently formalized FOCIL (EIP-7805) in Lean 4, and I'm using these first weeks to work out where that fits across the cohort projects. The FOCIL implementation work (Nimbus, Grandine) and Moonglass's Lean 4 track are the ones I'm looking at most closely so far.
Posted my week 0 update here: https://hackmd.io/@8t0zzURJSC6IX5BOuOiGqQ/Sk0vwhHZfg
Looking forward to working alongside you all.
